mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate sitting near zero (0.09%) reflects an accommodative monetary stance that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices as long as the rate remains near the lower bound. This is an always-on level effect independent of recent changes.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09 percent, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d return is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX returns are not significantly higher when FEDFUNDS is below 1% versus historical average (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 6.5%, it signals a labor market deterioration severe enough that equity markets price in reduced consumer spending and earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days until unemployment stabilizes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 6.5%, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of near-zero FEDFUNDS and elevated UNRATE reflects a policy environment where the central bank is actively stimulating a weak labor market; this combination signals forward-looking market pricing of a recovery-driven earnings rebound, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as stimulus transmits to the real economy. Neither series alone captures this expectation; the interaction of ultra-low rates with high unemployment is required.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following joint low-FEDFUNDS/high-UNRATE triggers are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing regime (FEDFUNDS near zero), rising HOUST housing starts reflect improving demand for durable goods financed by low mortgage rates, which leads consumer-sector earnings expectations higher and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon. This mechanism is regime-conditional: HOUST increases have weaker predictive power when rates are elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1415 thousand units, FEDFUNDS at 0.09
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with low rate environment
failure_condition: Positive HOUST momentum during low-rate regimes fails to produce forward 60-day SPX returns above the unconditional mean (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Short-term momentum in INDPRO (industrial production index) reflects real-time factory output trends that are rapidly incorporated into equity prices; positive month-over-month INDPRO growth predicts continued positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets extrapolate near-term supply-side strength.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant correlation (Rank IC < 0.02) with trailing INDPRO momentum over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in PAYEMS exceeding a critical threshold signals an abrupt deterioration in labor demand that historically precedes earnings recessions, causing S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days to turn negative as markets reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following PAYEMS decline triggers are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS (retail sales) reflects robust consumer spending that supports corporate revenue growth, producing a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to the latest consumption data release.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and forward 5-day SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation growth remains subdued while FEDFUNDS stays near zero, real interest rates are deeply negative, incentivizing capital flow into equities as a store of value; this joint low-inflation, low-rate condition produces sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon, but only during periods of accommodative policy - the effect reverses once the central bank begins tightening.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during negative real-rate regimes are not significantly different from baseline unconditional returns (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign once FEDFUNDS rises above 2% without corresponding hypothesis update.
